perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party will limit the unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information by using the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, as the Receiving Party uses to protect its own confidential information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party will limit the unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information by using the same degree of care, but no less than a commercially reasonable degree of care, as the Receiving Party uses to protect its own confidential Intellectual Property.
perturbation.explanation: The term “confidential information” is changed to “confidential Intellectual Property.” This creates ambiguity. Does it mean that all the confidential information needs to be classified as Intellectual Property, for it to be protected. This creates an internal contradiction of the Confidential Information as defined and the degree of care that the Receiving party will use to protect it. Since Intellectual Property may require further procedures to be regarded as the Receiving party's Intellectual Property.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term “Confidential Information” means all confidential and proprietary information belonging to one party (the “Disclosing Party”) that is revealed to the other party (the “Receiving Party”) during the Disclosure Period, which begins on the effective date and ends April 26, 2011. Confidential Information, regardless of the form in which it appears, may include, without limitation, information about customers, business and marketing plans, financial data, expertise of employees, forecasts and strategies that is related to the business of the Disclosing Party and is of the type of information that would reasonably be deemed to be confidential.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to "Confidential Information" as defined elsewhere in the contract. The changed text modifies this to "confidential Intellectual Property." The contradicted text provides a broad definition of "Confidential Information," including various types of data and expertise. By narrowing the scope to "confidential Intellectual Property," the changed text implies that only information classified as Intellectual Property is protected to the same degree. This creates a contradiction because the original definition of Confidential Information is much broader, and the Receiving Party may not be obligated to protect all forms of Confidential Information with the same degree of care, specifically if those are not also classified as Intellectual Property. Therefore, the change introduces a significant conflict between the changed text and the contradicted text, leading to ambiguity and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Except as permitted by the previous paragraph, neither the Receiving Party nor the Receiving Party’s representatives will disclose to any person the fact that the Confidential information has been made available to the Receiving Party or the Receiving Party’s representatives or that the Receiving Party or the Receiving Party’s representatives have inspected any portion of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as permitted by the previous paragraph, neither the Receiving Party nor the Receiving Party’s representatives will disclose to any entity the fact that the Confidential information has been made available to the Receiving Party or the Receiving Party’s representatives or that the Receiving Party or the Receiving Party’s representatives have inspected any portion of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “person” to “entity” creates a contradiction. 'Person' is a broader term, that can include individuals, while ‘entity’ is usually related to Companies. This change can be interpreted that it is allowed to disclose the confidential information to a single individual, but not to an entity. Thus creating a internal contradiction that could permit the Receiving Party to disclose the Confidential Information to an individual, that is not an entity, without being a breach of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party will disclose Confidential Information only to its employees and to its representative whose participation is necessary or helpful to evaluate and advance to the Business Transaction and who have agreed to be bound by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'person' to 'entity' in the Changed Text creates a contradiction with the Contradicted Text. The Contradicted Text specifies that Confidential Information can be disclosed to employees and representatives. The Changed Text now prohibits disclosure to 'any entity,' but arguably allows disclosure to individual people who are not entities. This contradicts the spirit and intent of the Contradicted Text, which assumes a controlled disclosure to specific individuals related to the company, leading to an inconsistency and potential breach of confidentiality if interpreted literally based on the modified wording. Thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the definitions provided.

perturbation.original_text: Because of the unique and proprietary nature of Confidential Information, it is understood and agreed that remedies at law for a breach by the Receiving Party of its obligations under this Agreement may be inadequate and that in the event of such breach or threatened breach the 1 Disclosing Party will be entitled to seek equitable relief, including without limitation injunctive relief and specific performance.
perturbation.changed_text: Because of the unique and proprietary nature of Confidential Information, it is understood and agreed that remedies at law for a breach by the Receiving Party of its obligations under this agreement may be capped to 10.000 USD and that in the event of such breach or threatened breach the 1 Disclosing Party will be entitled to seek equitable relief, including without limitation injunctive relief and specific performance.
perturbation.explanation: By adding an upper cap of 10.000 USD to the remedies at law in the situation of a breach, it completely contradicts that the remedies at law are inadequate and that the injured party will be entitled to seek equitable relief, including without limitation injunctive relief and specific performance, since, despite a breach happened, the law remedy is not adequate, but is limited and capped, so equitable reliefs cannot be enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Because of the unique and proprietary nature of Confidential Information, it is understood and agreed that remedies at law for a breach by the Receiving Party of its obligations under this agreement may be capped to 10.000 USD and that in the event of such breach or threatened breach the 1 Disclosing Party will be entitled to seek equitable relief, including without limitation injunctive relief and specific performance. The remedies set forth in this Section are in addition to any other remedies available for any such breach or threatened breach, including the recovery of damages from the breaching party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a cap of 10,000 USD to remedies at law, while the contradicted text states that the remedies in that section are in addition to any other remedies available. This creates a direct conflict because limiting the remedies at law contradicts the notion that other remedies, including equitable relief, are available without limitation if the legal remedy is deemed inadequate. The explanation states that capping the legal remedy contradicts the idea that equitable relief can be enforced since the legal remedy, while inadequate, is limited and capped, therefore, it does not open the possibility for equitable remedies.
